title: ServeMux Patterns, Precedence, Path Values, and Route Safety · Go Semantics Case: Pattern Precedence Conflict
summary: A Go 1.26 service mixes legacy prefix routes with method and wildcard patterns, conflicts at startup, misroutes escaped slashes, and assumes slash redirects still use a permanent status. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo pattern precedence conflict increment in validated ServeMux route and precedence table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive most-specific precedence as request-set inclusion and detect conflicting patterns before startup.
Author Go 1.26 ServeMux patterns with optional method, host, literal segments, single wildcards, remainder wildcards, and end markers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Pattern Precedence Conflict · Theory Pattern Precedence Conflict
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6967 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose a specific method-and-path route and preserve one registration owner; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive most-specific precedence as request-set inclusion and detect conflicting patterns before startup.
